entry.title: Re: Condition contract settlement using incorrect VAT cod...
entry.content:
The case was solved with the following change:

In the customizing step: SPRO - Logistics – general - Settlement management - Settlement documents - Settlement document types - All document types make sure that the field "Tax determination method" is set to "Transfer from pricing" on the settlement document type you use.

entry.content:

Thanks

]

entry.content: Good job

entry.title: INSERT FROM SELECT - The expression that contains "BUKRS" can get null values since it contains...
entry.content:
Hello,

I've got this ABAP syntax check warning for all key fields of ZTABLE (BUKRS, etc.) of below ABAP code:

The expression that contains "BUKRS" can get null values since it contains fields that were not created with "Initial Values" in the dictionary.

Syntax check internal message code: INSERT 211.

ABAP code (there's a list of columns and a WHERE clause with IN and = followed by ABAP variables, no SQL expression):

INSERT ztable FROM ( SELECT bukrs, ... FROM zcview ).

BUKRS is the first key field of ZTABLE. ZTABLE has a client key field in the very first position.

ZCVIEW is a CDS entity view with this code (only inner join, no left outer join):

define view entity ZCVIEW
  as select from bkpf
  inner join ...
{
  key bkpf.bukrs,
  ...
  sum( ... ) as sum_...,
  ...
  cast(' ' as belnr_d) as extbelnr,
  ...
}
where ... // conditions against constants
group by ... // all fields before the sum fields

The description says to use ##NULL_VALUES to remove the warning. This works fine.

I'd like to know the reason why there is this warning.

I see in the ABAP documentation INSERT dbtab, source - Alternative 3 ( SELECT subquery_clauses ... ):

Null values to be inserted can be produced in the following cases:

  • As results of outer joins in the FORM clause of the subquery.
  • As results of SQL expressions in the SELECT list of the subquery.
  • If a field read by the subquery already contains a null value.

A catchable exception of exception class CX_SY_OPEN_SQL_DB is raised in the case of columns that are key fields of the DDIC database table or DDIC view to be filled. If it is known statically that null values can be inserted into key fields, a syntax check warning that can be hidden by the pragma null_values is produced.

There is no reason that NULL values can be inserted in the column BUKRS of ZTABLE because the source is the key column BUKRS of BKPF.

Is there a way to avoid the warning without using ##NULL_VALUES?

In what situation is the warning not sent with INSERT ztable FROM ( SELECT ... )?

Thank you.

Sandra

ABAP release: 7.58.

entry.title: Re: SLT: Add field to target structure from logging table
entry.content:
I have now managed it. There is an old and a new loading mechanism.
You can switch to the old replication mechanism using report IUUC_REPL_SWITCH_REC_MCHNSM from note 3448296. The _it_io_s_logtab is then usually also available.
However, you should note that the old replication mechanism has some limitations, e.g. no CDS views can be used with it.
https://help.sap.com/doc/9d634180094347038e7b8c96661dc36b/latest/en-US/Compatibility_Views_and_SAP_LT_Replication_Server.pdf 
It would of course be great from SAP if the _it_io_s_logtab table was also filled with the new replication mechanism.

Translated with DeepL.com (free version)

entry.title: Re: Issue with Query Returning Incorrect Data from MARM T...
entry.content:
You are right. I have adjusted the code, but I am still not getting the desired result. The result remains the same.

DATA: lv_umren_ea TYPE P,
      lv_matnr TYPE MARM-MATNR.
CLEAR lv_umren_ea.
CLEAR lv_matnr.
SELECT SINGLE MATNR
INTO lv_matnr
FROM MARM
WHERE MARM~MEINH = 'EA'.
SELECT SINGLE MARM~UMREN
INTO lv_umren_ea
FROM MARM
JOIN MARC ON MARM~MATNR = MARC~MATNR
JOIN CAUFV ON MARC~WERKS = CAUFV~WERKS
WHERE MARM~MEINH = 'EA'
  AND MARM~MATNR = lv_matnr.
Z_EA = lv_umren_ea.

entry.title: Re: When uploading bank statement in FF_5, the items got...
entry.content:
Hi @kavmp ,

I'm afraid I can't help you with the details of your questions/issue. However, I'd just like to recommend you using the 3 options to import/upload bank statement files into your "SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition" system as described here: Overview on Automatic Bank Statement Processing 

The options exlained there are also the features that are getting actively developed. I'm afraid FF_5 is not one of them, and therefore we recommend switching to the other options. 

Best regards,

Anita

entry.title: Re: Outbound communication from SAP Public cloud to a Web...
entry.content:
> I will try the HTTP approach now even if that means i must abandon the Jco and implement a HTTP server.

Why that? What I meant is, that you can access the JCo Server via the SCC HTTP tunnel. (Therefore the "Allow WebSocket Upgrade" flag in SCC.)

The good thing about WebSocket-RFC is, that it can be tunneled via HTTP Proxy, and the combination of Connectivity Service + Cloud Connector technically just works like an HTTP Proxy. The only problem is, that I am not sure about, whether one can define an HTTP Proxy (hostname & port of the Connectivity Service) in the settings of the WebSocket-RFC destination in Steampunk. (In transaction SM59, destination of type W, you can.) I'll check with a Steampunk expert.

entry.content: Its how to get an information in tabled level for Material Additional Master Data

entry.title: Re: Field Control for Developer Extensibility Custom Fields
entry.content:
Hi @Junior_Abaper 

the determination of the mandatory property does not only happen, when opening the object page. However it depends on the app, when the field control properties are retrieved from the backend. This can also be controlled in the BDEF extension by defining side effects.

Example: as described in the blog, the field control for field ZZDeliveryUpdateMailAddressSDH can change to mandatory, when the field ZZPostponeDeliverySDH is set to true. Hence a side effect on the fieldZZPostponeDeliverySDH affecting the permissions of field ZZDeliveryUpdateMailAddressSDH needs to be defined. With that, the field control for field ZZDeliveryUpdateMailAddressSDH is reloaded whenever the field ZZPostponeDeliverySDH is changed.

Hope this helps 🙂

Daniel

Lead Architect - RAP Extensibility

entry.title: Re: PDF file upload for sales order
entry.content: Hi, if you want to add a PDF to the archive linked to a business object you can use ARCHIV_CREATE_OUTGOINGDOC_MULT; otherwise you can try to display the PDF out of S/4 via call of the respective API of the 3rd Party source system

Looking forward to hearing more!

How can i set up side effect to help it change feature real time base on source field value. In my case i want : When value of field A = 1, field B will be read-only, when value of field A = 2, it will be assigned value from field C , and when field A = 3, user can input free text here.

Hope get the help huhu. The feature of the field just re-set up when i f5(refresh) the object page .

entry.title: Re: How to use Notifications in SAP Cloud ALM?
entry.content:
Hi Peter,

until today it was not possible to get notifications for 'Involved Parties'.

What you could do was to use actively the 'Involved Parties' in filter criteria, save the views and set them as default in 'Tasks' App.

However there is a fresh update from today! 🙂

Please refer to 'What is New in SAP Cloud ALM':
https://help.sap.com/whats-new/0cb91c1ef73b400f881823f8d5fd2dea?locale=en-US

Kind regards,

Mariia

entry.title: Re: Issue with Query Returning Incorrect Data from MARM Table
entry.content: In your latest reply, you are using SELECT SINGLE from a JOIN/WHERE clause which extracts hundreds of lines, which means that you take any RANDOM line out of these hundreds of lines.

entry.title: Re: How to Automate Sales Order Quantity Based on Custom...
entry.content:
Hello,

To automate the sales order quantity based on data filled in custom fields within the sales order header in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, you can leverage in-app extensibility and custom logic implementation.

Step-by-Step Process

  1. Create Custom Fields

    • Access the ‘Custom Fields and Logic’ app under Extensibility. Here, you can create the necessary custom fields in the Sales Order header In-App Extensibility: Enhancing carrier informatio… that are required for automation.

     

  2. Implement Custom Logic

    • Use managed extensibility to implement the custom logic needed In-App Extensibility: Enhancing carrier informatio… to automate the sales order quantity based on the values of custom fields. This logic can be crafted using Business Add-Ins (BAdIs) which will trigger on certain business contexts, such as sales order header.

    • You might start by selecting the relevant business context in which the custom logic needs to be executed, e.g., Sales Order Header Modification.

  3. Automation Implementation

    • Define and implement the logic that adjusts the sales order quantity based on the input in custom fields. This could involve calculations or conditional checks.

     

    • Ensure the logic gets triggered during the process of saving or modifying the sales order.

  4. Testing and Validation

    • After implementing the logic, thoroughly test the automation to confirm that the sales order quantity changes appropriately based on the data entered into the custom fields.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Create Button on Purchase Order
entry.content:
Hi

   I have below code to connect to a Company. Now want to create a button in Purchase Order form as Send Email.

oCompany = new SAPbobsCOM.Company();
oCompany.Server = "NDB@10.0.1.28:30015";
oCompany.DbServerType = BoDataServerTypes.dst_HANADB;
oCompany.CompanyDB = "LIVE_Database";
oCompany.UserName = "manager";
oCompany.Password = "123";
oCompany.language = BoSuppLangs.ln_English;
oCompany.UseTrusted = true;

int connectionResult = oCompany.Connect();
if (connectionResult != 0)
{
throw new Exception("Error connecting to SAP Business One: " + oCompany.GetLastErrorDescription());
}

Thanks

entry.title: SAP Solution Roadmap for Oil, Gas, and Energy 2025 Updates
entry.content:
Greetings,

This blog post highlights SAP's Oil, Gas, and Energy solution portfolio roadmap, focusing specifically on innovations and development enabling customers to be their best in the Upstream, Downstream, and Commodity Management space of their business.

SAP's continued collaboration with the industry to build a solution and bring innovations built with the customer and for the customer in the Upstream area of Field Logistics is facilitating the delivery of capabilities and functionality that bring tremendous value to customers managing and executing on the end-to-end process of getting equipment and material to places where work needs to be done and back to where it needs to be once work has been completed.  Through the active engagement of the Field Logistics worskstream, the FPS02 release of Field Logistics is rich and comprehensive in capabilities, functionality, and innovations, making it the minimum release for customers to begin their journey with Field Logistics.  The recent release of FPS03 brings even more capabilities with the addition of use cases related to Goods Receipt, management of Single Plant scenarios, and added features and functions associated with Kitting at remote sites.  Moreover, AI use cases and mobility scenarios are being introduced and released, adding more simplicity and efficiency to the end-to-end process of managing and executing field logistic activities. The following depicts the roadmap for Field Logistics:

Additionally, within the Hydrocarbon Accounting and Management area of Upstream substantial focus has been placed on enabling Clean Core through the release of API's/CDS Views, facilitating accurate and simplified Compliance Reporting, and making automation a more common part of the Production and Revenue accounting monthly and prior-period processing.   A recap of what has been done and preview of what is planned to be done for Upstream Hydrocarbon Accounting and Management exists on the following image:

Within Downstream, several innovations and development items are inflight or planned, as depicted in the diagrams that follow this paragraph.  Most of these are driven by the objective to bridge gaps with other SAP products that are currently available or have been deprecated in the past years. Providing more comprehensive coverage of requirements and enabling an optimized solution landscape is the goal of roadmap activity in the Downstream space.  Presently, Secondary Distribution is most dynamically bringing innovations and building out a comprehensive and capability enriched roadmap.  The following depicts the roadmap for Downstream:

 

Lastly, as it relates to Commodity Management, a board sponsored and budget approved 4-year investment roadmap is being realized, enabling SAP to provide an end-to-end commodity management solution that delivers value-add to our customers and facilitates their move to RISE (incl. AI adoption).  The diagram that follows depicts that roadmap.  Included in that roadmap is an AI use case expected to be released by those of 2025.  AI Assisted Commodity WorkCenter (ACW), which will streamline key front office processes, includes enhanced deal capture capabilities supported by Joule and GenAI.   This roadmap represents significant investment in strong cross industry capabilities, affecting both SAP CM and SAP ACM) which mean increased adoption potential for every $/€ spent, whilst also assisting our customers with their transition to cleaner and more sustainable supply chain portfolios.

A big THANK YOU to Richard Philcox (richard.philcox@sap.com), Jean-Marc DELBOS, (jean-marc.delbos@sap.com), Diana Temirbulatova (diana.temirbulatova@sap.com), and Jythois Sasidhara Varier (Jyothis.sasidhara.varier@sap.com) for their support and input on the Commodity Management, Downstream, and Upstream roadmap content, respectively.

A big THANK YOU to you as well for taking the time to get updated on the roadmap for SAP Oil, Gas, and Energy.

entry.title: SAP Business One SDK - LoadFromDataSource(), FlushToDataSource(), how to store data back to UDT?
entry.content:
I've created my own User-defined table and use it in my editable matrix sending data back and forth to DBDataSource with LoadFromDataSource() and FlushToDataSource()

The question is, to store data, including insert, update and delete, back to UDT, do I need to manually loop over rows in the matrix, then run .Update(), .Add() or .Remove() myself or is there a way to manage it automatically?

I'm stuck at FlushToDataSource() after clicked on a Update button and ChatGPT taught me to manually set the data back, but I don't think it's an efficient way and tried to find other ways in UI and DI manual but not found anything.

Here is my code

if (pVal.ItemUID == "1" && pVal.FormMode == (int)SAPbouiCOM.BoFormMode.fm_UPDATE_MODE && pVal.EventType == SAPbouiCOM.BoEventTypes.et_ITEM_PRESSED && pVal.BeforeAction == true)
{
    SAPbouiCOM.Form oForm = oApp.Forms.Item(pVal.FormUID);
    SAPbouiCOM.Matrix oMatrix = (SAPbouiCOM.Matrix)oForm.Items.Item("matrix").Specific;
                        
    oMatrix.FlushToDataSource();

    SAPbouiCOM.DBDataSource oDBDataSource = oForm.DataSources.DBDataSources.Item("@myUDT");
                        
    ...here is where I'm stuck.

}

entry.title: Re: display the value of a global variable in a popup win...
entry.content:
As per your code, Global variable gets updated based on count in the loop. 
However, your Application/Widget (Front end view) is not refreshed to display the updated value in Global variable.
In my opinion, if you refresh for every count, it will impact the performance.

entry.title: Re: SAP Roadmap available in CALM but not in Roadmap Viewer?
entry.content:
Hi Karola,

in case you have the Brownfield Conversion to SAP S/4 HANA (without migration to Cloud or RISE with SAP), please take a look to the following roadmap:

Here is a link to a Roadmap Viewer: https://me.sap.com/roadmapviewer/group/AAE80671-5087-430B-9AA7-8FBE881CF548/roadmapoverviewpage/S4HANATRANSONPRE

entry.title: Re: HTTP response '502: Bad Gateway. Unable to open conne...
entry.content:
Have a look here: https://ga.support.sap.com/dtp/viewer/index.html#/tree/2757/actions/39812:39813:39818:49938

HTTP 502 error 

CIG has successfully reached your server, however, it has responded with an Error - HTTP 502 - The server, while acting as a gateway or proxy, received an invalid response from an inbound server that it accessed while attempting to fulfill the request

Answer

After Cloud Connector version 12.13.2 you will find issues related to the 502 error on CIG.

When checking the Cloud Connector logs, you can see following error:

com.sap.core.connectivity.protocol.http.handlers.HttpProtocolException: Invalid server certificate

This is happening due to new security improvements added by 12.13.2, wherein case there is any mismatch between the internal host configured in the CC and the ERP certificate, the error will be triggered.

Follow below KBA from BC-MID-SCC component that explains how to solve the issue:

Invalid server certificate error after cloud connector upgrade to 2.13.2

entry.title: Not able to insert/delete records in database table
entry.content: Executing "DELETE <dbtab>" from "SELECT" violates transactional contract "READ". getting this error when trying to delete entries from a custom database table in an unmanaged query class implementation.

I'm not sure if it was moved or removed from the system or even there are updated training manuals. I'm using SAP B11000-2502-70004131 (SQL) and the training Manuals where downloaded from the learning hub. (screens attached).

From the HELP Portal I get the same information as on the training Manual. I'm training an internal team and I don't know what to do.

Your help will be much appreciated.

Thank you in advance

Santos Joao

entry.title: Cloud ALM and SNOW integration - create incident for red alerts only
entry.content:
Hi,

We are currently integrating SAP Cloud ALM and ServiceNow and have adjusted our mappings. The incident create and ping event situations are working. However, a new incident is getting created for the same alert multiple times. For e.g, 

  • There is a yellow alert raised for 'Hana Memory' metric --> an incident is created.
  • The alert rating changes from yellow to red --> a new incident gets created.

How can we adjust the mapping (what condition to add and how) such that incident is only raised when alert rating is red? no incidents are required for yellow rating alerts.

entry.title: Migrate your code with UI5 linter – Autofix has landed
entry.content:
UI5 linter is the tool of choice to check your UI5 project for using best practices and replacement of deprecated UI5 APIs. Last year, we focused on the detection capabilities of UI5 linter. In early 2025, we started on an actual migration functionality to be offered by UI5 linter, a.k.a "Autofix". Right from the beginning, our goal was to provide a similar developer experience as offered by the famous ESLint project. With UI5 linter v1.12.0, we are happy to announce the initial version of an autofix feature. You can directly use the "--fix" argument to let UI5 linter apply fixes to your code automatically.

Feature

In our initial autofix version, UI5 linter can replace the deprecated usage of UI5 globals with our recommended way of importing the respective module and using the local name in your code.

Let’s try it out! In our application we have used the deprecated global notation when creating an sap.ui.model.json.JSONModel instance:

 

sap.ui.define([
  "sap/ui/core/mvc/Controller"
], (Controller) => {
  "use strict";

  return Controller.extend("ui5.app.controller.Home", {
    onInit() {
      this.getView().setModel(new sap.ui.model.json.JSONModel({
        achievement: "UI5 linter Autofix has landed"
      }));
    }
  });
});

 

Now, let’s execute UI5 linter to see whether it detects the deprecated usage of the JSON model. Simply call "ui5lint" in a shell of your choice.

 

> ui5lint
UI5 linter report:

/ui5-app/webapp/controller/Home.controller.js
  8:41 error Access of global variable 'sap' (sap.ui.model.json.JSONModel)  no-globals

1 problems (1 errors, 0 warnings)
  Run 'ui5lint --fix' to resolve all auto-fixable problems

Note: Run 'ui5lint --details' to get more information on the findings

 

Got it! UI5 linter has found the issue. We could fix the issue on our own or we can let UI5 linter do the job for us by executing "ui5lint --fix".

 

> ui5lint --fix
UI5 linter report:


0 problems (0 errors, 0 warnings)

 

Yay! UI5 linter says it was able to fix my code. Let’s see whether UI5 linter is right:

 

sap.ui.define([
  "sap/ui/core/mvc/Controller",
  "sap/ui/model/json/JSONModel"
], (Controller, JSONModel) => {
  "use strict";

  return Controller.extend("ui5.app.controller.Home", {
    onInit() {
      this.getView().setModel(new JSONModel({
        achievement: "UI5 linter Autofix has landed"
      }));
    }
  });
});

 

There are plenty of other deprecation types waiting to be fixed automatically by UI5 linter, and our team puts a huge focus on giving you an update here soon. See our roadmap https://github.com/SAP/ui5-linter/issues/323 to get an overview of our planned upcoming features.

entry.title: Re: Clarification on Work Clearance Management (WCM) in S...
entry.content:
Greetings,

the solution is integrateed to SAP EAM (Plant maintenance) but I think it is subject to additional licence (I am not sure regarding that).

Based on the proces you provided I think WCM is the solution to be used. I think you should familiarize yourself with official SAP Help which is sometimes a bit difficult to understand so I would suggest the following additional resources:

https://www.slideshare.net/slideshow/sap-pm-wcm-enhanced-model-entire-process-flow-with-sap-screensh...

https://www.wcm-it.com/english/product/processes/

Best regards, Matjaž.

entry.title: Re: External system SAP Localization Hub Advanced Compliance Reporting (SLHACR)

entry.title: Re: Material PO text not visible in PO Print Preview
entry.content: Hi All, Once we click on this button after saving it. Changes will reflect for every user or every user need to click this button. And we need click this button one time and save?

entry.title: Restricting transaction codes IW31, IW32, and IW33 at the order type level
entry.content:
Hi,

We are trying to restrict transaction codes IW31, IW32, and IW33 at the order type level. However, these transactions invoke the same authorization object, I_AUART, which contains only two authorization fields: WERKS and AUFART.

Could you confirm if there is an existing standard solution available for this requirement?

Looking forward to your response.

Thank you.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Restricting transaction codes IW31, IW32, and IW33 at...
entry.content:
BADI IWO1_ORDER_BADI with method AUTHORITY_CHECK_AUART_ACTIVIT was developed for this purpose

With this BADI you can restrict the order type for a certain plant and a certain activity (Add/Change/Display):

It offers the follwing parameters for the check:

AUART_IMP = Order type of the order

IWERK_IMP = Maintenance planning plant

ACTIVITY_IMP = Activity category (H = Add, V = Change, A = Display)

Please see the documentation in transaction SE18 for further information.

entry.title: Re: First look into SAP Datasphere's new version control functionality

Thanks Simon! 🙂

Cool feature!

Happy to see the investments in there!

entry.title: Re: DBSQL_FAE_STATEMENT_TO_BIG - SELECT with FOR ALL ENTRIES
entry.content: I would suggest performing the selection without the condition for fistl and then checking fistl in a loop over the result table.

entry.title: Re: Making SAP Business One Web Client accessible externally using nginx as a reverse proxy
entry.content:
Hi，Guy_Sujetzki

After I set according to your steps, when I access the web client or controlcenter, the web page will automatically jump to the auth page indicating that I cannot log in. I found in the conf file that the port of my b1as is 40021. But I am going to add the b1c_extAddress.conf, upstream B1as{
server 127.0.0.1:40021;
} Set 40021 is also invalid

First, the requested login address is not port 40021, and then I manually access the 40021 request verification, but I am prompted that the certificate is invalid, I want to ask should I let the login request address automatically jump to port 40021, and how to set the correct certificate

entry.title: Error connecting Datasphere as a datasource in Snowflake
entry.content:
Hello,

We are trying to connect SAP Datasphere as a datasource in Snowflake, for this we are following these steps:

On Datasphere side:

  1. Create a DB hana user in Datasphere space management.
  2. Add the snowflake IP in the Datasphere's IP allowlist.
  3. Add snowflake as a trusted origins in the app integration from Datasphere's system administration.

On Snowflake side:

  1. Install SAP HANA JDBC driver (versión ngdbc-2.23.10).
  2. Create a network rule with the hostname of Datasphere DB.
  3. Configure the Secret to hold the credentials of Datasphere hana DB user.
  4. Create external access integration.
  5. Create a function to connect to datasphere.

After completing these steps, we make a select to one view created in datasphere with the expose for Consumption using the Function created in snowflake and we get the following error:

{ "ERROR": "com.sap.db.jdbc.exceptions.SQLInvalidAuthorizationSpecExceptionSapDB: [10]: authentication failed: Detailed info for this error can be found with correlation ID 'C72F4380CAAEB647B6A3F49747F9F11D'" }

Could anybody advise how to resolve this error?

 

Many thanks and kind regards.

SAP Datasphere #Snowflake

entry.title: Re: Equal Workload distribution to HANA Scale out database servers.
entry.content:
Hi,

IMO, it's important to distinguish whether you're really issuing a certain SQL query or not.

That is, if your concern is to distribute your workloads evenly to your multiple HANA instances regardless of which SQL query runs at which instance, the answer would be totally different.

 

Assuming that you concern about a certain SQL query only, usually there are some conditions to determine where the SQL query to run.

For example, if this SQL query is to select from table A, obviously this query cannot run at other instances except the HANA instance where table A is stored.

So, plz check if the database artifacts of your SQL query are distributed over multiple HANA instances or not.

If not, you might resolve your issue by distributing it (e.g., change the table to partitioned table, then distribute the partitions evenly to your HANA instances).

Hope it helps.

BR, Jaeyoung

entry.title: Re: How to Update DMEE Format CGI_XML_CT to the pain 001....

entry.title: Re: Mass Maintenance - Products taking a long time
entry.content:
Hello,

Common cause could be due to custom logic.

You can try to check whether there is implementation which can cause performance issue.

If it's not the case, it's necessary to collect performance trace to find out the reason.

Best Regards,
Owen

entry.title: Why the expiry date of SF "Certified Professional" in Credly is not updated after Stay Current test
entry.content:
I have completed the SuccessFactors Stay Current assessment in Jan 2025.

The expiry date of "SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant" is updated for anothter year to Jan 2026 in Credly after the assessment is taken.

However, the expiry date of "SAP Certified Professional Application Consultant" is still shown as "Mar 2025".

What should I do to get this fixed? Thanks a lot.

entry.title: Dynamic paragraph on Offer Letter
entry.content:
We are using the new functionality release with 2H 2024 to create dynamic paragraph in offer letter trhough business rules but it seems that it0s not possible to create several rules for one single template.

Is that correct? it's impossible to have just one rule due to our template complexity, we have several dynamic paragraphs and several combinantion on them, this should means create undreds of IF on the same rule and it's tricky in terms of implementation and also for future maintenance.

Is there any workaround?

entry.title: Implementing Message Level Security: End-to-End Encryption and Decryption in SAP CPI
entry.content:
Introduction

In our previous blog, we explored the creation and configuration of PGP keys in SAP CPI. This time, we'll dive into the end-to-end implementation and working of the encryptor and decryptor, along with the sign/verify mechanism of the payload. We'll use the Postman tool to test the scenario and utilize the same keys from our previous blog on creating and managing PGP key pairs for SAP CPI integration.

Overview

This blog will cover the following steps:

  • Interface Design Implementation
  • Encrypting and Signing the Plain Text Payload
  • Decrypting and Verifying the Payload to Retrieve Plain Text

     

    Step 1: Interface Design Implementation

    First, we need to implement the interface design. This involves setting up the necessary configurations in SAP CPI to handle the encryption and decryption processes. We will use two different interfaces for encryption and decryption:

    • Encryption and Signing Interface: In the first interface, named"Z_DEMO_PGP_Encrypt_SIGN", we will encrypt and sign the data coming from Postman using the client partner's public key and our own private key.
    • Decryption and Verification Interface: In the second interface, named "ZDEMOPGPDecryptorVerifier", we will decrypt and verify the outcome of the first interface using the partner's private key and our own public key.

      Note: We don't have a configuration for the PGP decryptor pallet, so we don't need to provide the private key alias. However, the private key should be deployed in Manage PGP Keys. If the incoming payload is signed, we need to provide the public key details for verifying the signature.

      Step 2: Encrypting and Signing the Plain Text Payload

      Next, we'll use the encryptor interface to encrypt and sign the plain text payload data. This step ensures that the data is securely transmitted and can be verified for integrity. Below are the configuration details and input/output samples from Postman:

      Configuration Details:

      • Encryptor Interface: "Z_DEMO_PGP_Encrypt_SIGN"
      • Public Key Alias: PartnerDevKey  (for encryption)
      • Private Key Alias: CpiDemoKey     (for Signer)

  

  

 

 

Postman Input Sample:

Hello from Postman

Postman Output Sample:

-----BEGIN PGP MESSAGE-----

wcBMA0cyi8mLJEMqAQf+JuSjk8UxfScdIhJt1Ginupw0pSW+9rojhYZUgFXbxrFm
MtOxGsUbGgpkIqzGp0LdVUPnv+fkjt1uvDcdK/V78CMDEpmA3TRz+Jz6c3JP7wfp
rPN7kEW8O94kPm/h34HKu+hDQMConSUnKPfnuMYLfcnYeHHPfPzwUum2531/HGou
Ap3wUyOPHcJR/IABtxlMnA0hs33xsdmRUgK9AS3kUPPt6mybLc+x7TL3JNnLNxSS
XBcBw4QYc02cnFLl984w/64U16fwiut1AuqE7FluJvlvZBNn0Xrxg3OQelg8SIFq
cZrw8pnxa3P8S7Z8VAzGTKKALcA9F0QBDjw+/N+wV9LA2AGsM8fDpInVitZRGAfk
i2kWR4eMmmxtMg7Ek8RGlZFpKWvAur3hZOp2VkSQkCaioigfkP34H6tKheWCfaIV
YaTbQvgKgN3Y15Cg8/0bVisYOjiEv9L8FMNihRmi5D7em+oOFXhtrMVRRIi33q9s
w0Nz0OYezC7jomXd01xS9GHtnk6yqhVlB+uN+2x8RzNbQbsFHG+eaP1jrnQWcVXa
3FLGsBjsXoVyPseCk1j8k6t/DMO/O/XKdUTppC3TH1W7JaTW9gozNCuogX7sHXAC
kJx/uXqlBei6cLgkUF1n53PDQLPfLwB49n12zi6eF8OVc0K9Zv6iHcKltSbFy6+R
eqQpO7Aib99Ew4unMjSMd+cJQwzSxwInkYsRECQLG19QTo+k0OamCtMWmON/mRqX
PDT/xEKdv7Cp1huEFHBoOfQmSSjN9k8fTSfIX1FihxdFbtks3yE5jHVgm4JS7Dbb
OeyWcw3n3YlTh7pBE2EOGvrMQmzUTe28V9uKVr+MJwnX1Py+yeCv44ho+z4eN0GL
3o8w5Oy1exww0Q==
=03nq
-----END PGP MESSAGE-----

 

 

 

Using Postman, we can test this scenario by sending a plain text payload to the encryptor interface and observing the encrypted and signed output.

Step 3: Decrypting and Verifying the Payload to Retrieve Plain Text

Finally, we'll send the output from the encryptor interface to the decryptor interface. This step will decrypt the payload and verify its integrity, ensuring that the original plain text data is retrieved accurately. Below are the configuration details and input/output samples from Postman:

Configuration Details:

  • Decryptor Interface: "Z_DEMO_PGP_Decryptor_Verifier"
  • Private Key Alias: No configuration step available ( it must deployed in Manage PGP Keys)
  • Public Key Alias: CpiDemoKey (for verifying the signature)

 

         In PGP decryptor's processing, we have Three options to handle Signature.

          1. None Expected.

          2. Optional.

          3. Required.

           

   Here, in content modifier add  "Below Message is decrypted and verified." with decrypted payload.

   This step is optional.

 

Postman Input Sample:

Output of previous step.

-----BEGIN PGP MESSAGE-----

wcBMA0cyi8mLJEMqAQf+JuSjk8UxfScdIhJt1Ginupw0pSW+9rojhYZUgFXbxrFm
MtOxGsUbGgpkIqzGp0LdVUPnv+fkjt1uvDcdK/V78CMDEpmA3TRz+Jz6c3JP7wfp
rPN7kEW8O94kPm/h34HKu+hDQMConSUnKPfnuMYLfcnYeHHPfPzwUum2531/HGou
Ap3wUyOPHcJR/IABtxlMnA0hs33xsdmRUgK9AS3kUPPt6mybLc+x7TL3JNnLNxSS
XBcBw4QYc02cnFLl984w/64U16fwiut1AuqE7FluJvlvZBNn0Xrxg3OQelg8SIFq
cZrw8pnxa3P8S7Z8VAzGTKKALcA9F0QBDjw+/N+wV9LA2AGsM8fDpInVitZRGAfk
i2kWR4eMmmxtMg7Ek8RGlZFpKWvAur3hZOp2VkSQkCaioigfkP34H6tKheWCfaIV
YaTbQvgKgN3Y15Cg8/0bVisYOjiEv9L8FMNihRmi5D7em+oOFXhtrMVRRIi33q9s
w0Nz0OYezC7jomXd01xS9GHtnk6yqhVlB+uN+2x8RzNbQbsFHG+eaP1jrnQWcVXa
3FLGsBjsXoVyPseCk1j8k6t/DMO/O/XKdUTppC3TH1W7JaTW9gozNCuogX7sHXAC
kJx/uXqlBei6cLgkUF1n53PDQLPfLwB49n12zi6eF8OVc0K9Zv6iHcKltSbFy6+R
eqQpO7Aib99Ew4unMjSMd+cJQwzSxwInkYsRECQLG19QTo+k0OamCtMWmON/mRqX
PDT/xEKdv7Cp1huEFHBoOfQmSSjN9k8fTSfIX1FihxdFbtks3yE5jHVgm4JS7Dbb
OeyWcw3n3YlTh7pBE2EOGvrMQmzUTe28V9uKVr+MJwnX1Py+yeCv44ho+z4eN0GL
3o8w5Oy1exww0Q==
=03nq
-----END PGP MESSAGE-----

Postman Output Sample:

Below Message is decrypted and verified.
Hello from Postman

Using Postman, we can verify that the decrypted data matches the original payload, confirming the process works correctly.

Conclusion

By following these steps, you can successfully implement and test the encryptor and decryptor mechanisms in SAP CPI. This ensures secure and verified data transmission, leveraging the PGP keys created earlier. Stay tuned for more insights and detailed implementations in our upcoming blogs!

 

Feel free to leave any questions, suggestions, or feedback in the comments section below. We'd love to hear from you!

entry.title: Re: Joule for Developers: Word Search Challenge
entry.content: First that I saw was UNITTESTGENERATION. I assume that Joule will prepare unit test data, test scenarios and do the tests.

entry.title: Re: ASE 16.1 didn't start as a windows service
entry.content: I tried it with both existing and new customers, but it didn’t help. Is there a way to drop and recreate the ASE Windows service only without dropping and recreating the ASE server itself?

entry.title: New "My Team Calendar (S/4HANA)" and Overlap Calender for My Leave Requests/Approve Leave Requests
entry.content:
Hi Experts, we have just implemented the new "My Team Calender" 1309B. However, when using My Inbox/Leave requests the old overlap calender is shown. 

The App Documentations states: "Define Usage And Assign Settings: The team calendar can be used in several Fiori apps, e.g. My Team Calendar or My Leave Request. You can define the usage of the team calendar and assign a setting ID. The setting ID is used in the other customizing activities to determine the settings for your app."

However, we haven't found any customizing activities for this and also when debugging we haven't found a way so switch to the new Team Calender.

Has anyome already implemented the new app and has more information?

Regards

Julia

I have made an effort to find a solution, but it seems that the system has been upgraded, rendering the previous methods inapplicable. Could you kindly assist me in resolving this issue?
I sincerely appreciate your support.

entry.title: Re: Define Priorities in Maintenance Management, S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition
entry.content: add

entry.title: Re: How to use Smart Link in Fiori Elements
entry.content:
Hi,

When using Fiori Elements, the app is primarily metadata-driven, which explains why you don't see a views.xml file. Instead, you're working with an annotations.xml file.

To add a second semantic object for app navigation, "AccountingDocument", please try the below:-

1) Create a view.xml and corresponding controller holding the 

<SmartLink text="{Name}" semanticObject="dummy" additionalSemanticObjects="demokit_smartlink_example_09_SemanticObjectNameAdditional,demokit_smartlink_example_09_SemanticObjectNameAdditional2" enableAvailableActionsPersonalization="false"/>

2) Update the manifest.json

"routes": [
    {
        "pattern": "account",
        "name": "AccountView",
        "target": "AccountView"
    }
],
"targets": {
    "AccountView": {
        "viewType": "XML",
        "viewName": "<namespace>.view.AccountDocument"
    }
}

This way we can add custom UI elements (like smartLink) beyond what annotations provide.

entry.title: Re: Reading a property in Content Filter
entry.content:
Hello,

This is the XPath expression to read property in Content Filter

/OnboardingCandidateInfo/OnboardingCandidateInfo[(contains($fName,fName))]

Here we access the property fName with a $ preceding it.

Regards,

Varun Vichare.

entry.title: Create condition table for tax code or tax procedure
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I would like to automatically enter a tax code in the "Invoice" tab of a purchase order.

I need to create a new condition table, which I will associate with an "Access Sequence" (OBQ2), which is associated with a condition type (OBQ1) which is associated with a tax procedure (OBQ3)

But I can't find the transaction allowing me to create a condition table for tax codes.

I know the transactions M/03 (Create condition table Pricing Purchasing) and V/03 (Create condition table Sales/Distribution). These transactions don't allow me to create a condition table for tax codes and associate it with a tax type condition (OBQ2)

Can you please tell me which transaction allows me to "Create Condition table for TaxCode or Tax Procedure"?

 

Thank you for your help,

Regards,

Max

entry.title: Re: Profit center and company code Unassignment mass change
entry.content:
Hi @medkhaled ,

You can use Tcode KE56 (manual) or program RCOPCA60_2 (automatic) for mass assignment of profit center and company code.

I hope it helps.

BR,

Filipe

entry.title: "Service Partner User Cockpit" access card
entry.content:
Hi all,

I am missing the "Service Partner User Cockpit" access card in SAP for ME in the page "Users & Contacts". 

When entering the relevant URL "https://me.sap.com/app/spuc" I can access the page and also see open invitations from customers and I can see the existing Customer relationships.

How can I get the "Service Partner User Cockpit" access card in SAP for ME? From my understanding, if it is not assigned to my SAP for ME account I would also not have access to the above URL.

Thx

Edgar

entry.title: Re: Business partner **** is not blocked; is used in syst...
entry.content:
Hi @Phil_P 

It seems that the main purpose here is to delete unused workforce data.

There are three sets of data (work assignment, business user and business partner), also there are severaly steps need to be follow.

May I know if you checked steps in SAP Help Portal - Delete Employee Data. In which step did you stuck?

Please note that ILM process is not for a specific worker but for archiving or deleting all the workforce persons who are terminated in the system.

Best regards,

Irene

entry.title: Re: Italy eDocument for zero value invoices
entry.content: Thank you, this worked. The 100% discount invoice eDocument was processed.

entry.title: Re: Set G/L Accounts by in the latest SAP Business One re...
entry.content:
Hi,

A matter of language settings. By the look of your screenshot you have set your system to "English (United Kingdom)", whereas the documentation was apparently written according to "English (United States)".

Set your system to US english, and it will look like this:

Or look for "Set Stock Method By".

Regards,

Johan

entry.title: Using Ctrl + P on SAP B1 Web Browser
entry.content:
Dear  Expert,

Can we use shortcut key as Ctrl + P , Ctrl + S for Layout printing on Web browser?

Best Regards,
Peearput
